mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, and PDGFR kinases to block proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Particularly relevant for FLT3-ITD+ AML such as MOLM13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR, and VEGFR kinases
additional_notes: Has anti-proliferative effects in AML cell lines

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and Src family kinases, disrupting survival signaling
Inhibits BCR-ABL with off-target inhibition of KIT and PDGFR
additional_notes: Some reported activity in AML models

category: BCR-ABL, KIT, PDGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, KIT, and PDGFR signaling pathways
additional_notes: Active in KIT-mutated myeloid disease; partial effects in some AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits ALK, MET, and ROS1 receptor tyrosine kinases
additional_notes: Targets MET/ALK pathways that can support AML cell survival

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton’s tyrosine kinase, blocking BCR-like signaling
additional_notes: Emerging data for activity in some myeloid/AML contexts

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti-apoptotic protein BCL-2 to induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for AML; MOLM13 is often highly sensitive

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to block JAK/STAT cytokine signaling
additional_notes: Relevant for cytokine-driven or JAK/STAT-dependent AML subsets

mechanism: Inhibits JAK family kinases to reduce JAK/STAT pathway signaling
Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and disrupts NPL4/proteasome function, affecting proteostasis
additional_notes: Activity often enhanced with 1–2 µM CuCl2; repurposed anticancer candidate

mechanism: Activates AMPK and modulates mTOR and cellular metabolism
additional_notes: Anti-leukemic effects typically at low-mM in vitro concentrations

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal acidification and autophagic flux
additional_notes: Hydroxychloroquine is an alternative with similar autophagy-inhibiting mechanism

mechanism: Blocks β1 and β2 adrenergic receptors, altering adrenergic signaling
additional_notes: Adrenergic signaling can influence leukemia cell survival and niche interactions

mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, disrupting mevalonate pathway and prenylation
additional_notes: Simvastatin or atorvastatin can be used as alternatives, often effective at 0.01–10 µM

mechanism: Binds cereblon, altering ubiquitination and degradation of transcription factors and modulating immune/microenvironmental signaling
additional_notes: Used with lenalidomide as related IMiDs

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing ROS and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Reported pro-oxidant and cytotoxic effects in AML cells

mechanism: Binds tubulin and disrupts microtubule polymerization
additional_notes: Also reported to affect hedgehog signaling; preclinical AML data exist

mechanism: Acts as a mitochondrial uncoupler and inhibits Wnt/β-catenin and STAT3 signaling
additional_notes: Has broad anti-cancer activity in vitro

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial function, increases ROS, and affects ion channels
additional_notes: Demonstrated anti-leukemic activity in preclinical models, targeting oxidative metabolism
